sections.title: Promise Statement
sections.content:
> **"Deliver precision steel cross-sections with controlled geometry, dimensional accuracy, surface quality, and mechanical properties — configured to application requirements."**

This is the universal functional promise of the ZOLLERN Steel Profiles capability platform. It is derived from the core mechanism (plastic deformation through shaped dies/rolls) and the configurable feature envelope.

sections.title: Promise Decomposition
sections.content:
The functional promise decomposes into five independently measurable sub-promises:

| Sub-Promise | Metric | Range | Why It Matters |
|-------------|--------|-------|---------------|
| Geometric fidelity | Profile deviation from design | +/- 0.02 to +/- 0.5 mm | Determines fit in mating assemblies |
| Dimensional consistency | Bar-to-bar variation | Cpk >= 1.33 | Enables batch processing without individual inspection |
| Surface integrity | Roughness Ra, defect rate | Ra 0.4 - 6.3 um | Affects sealing, wear, fatigue life |
| Mechanical property control | Yield strength, hardness | 350 - 2,100 MPa / 0-64 HRC | Ensures structural performance in end-use |
| Material efficiency | Buy-to-fly ratio | 0.85 - 0.98 | Near-net-shape minimizes customer machining waste |

sections.title: Promise vs. Alternatives
sections.content:
The functional promise gains meaning when compared against alternative manufacturing approaches:

| Alternative | Geometric Fidelity | Material Efficiency | Lead Time | Cost at Volume | Best For |
|------------|-------------------|-------------------|-----------|---------------|---------|
| **ZOLLERN profile** | HIGH | 0.85-0.98 | 8-16 wks (1st), days (repeat) | LOW | Repeating cross-sections, medium-high volume |
| Machined bar stock | VERY HIGH | 0.20-0.60 | 1-4 wks | HIGH | Prototypes, very low volume |
| Open-die forging | LOW | 0.40-0.70 | 6-12 wks | MEDIUM | Large, 3D shapes |
| Investment casting | HIGH | 0.85-0.95 | 10-20 wks | MEDIUM | Complex 3D, low volume |
| 3D printing (DMLS) | MEDIUM | 0.90-0.99 | 1-2 wks | VERY HIGH | Prototypes, topology-optimized |
| Extrusion (aluminum) | HIGH | 0.90-0.98 | 4-8 wks | LOW | Non-ferrous only |

sections.title: Promise Boundary Conditions
sections.content:
The promise holds **only within** these boundary conditions:

| Condition | Constraint | Implication |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| 2D geometry | Cross-section constant along length | Cannot produce 3D-varying geometry (e.g., tapered, twisted) |
| Steel only | No aluminum, titanium, brass, copper | Excludes lightweight and corrosion-critical applications requiring non-ferrous |
| Minimum batch | ~100 kg cold drawn, ~500 kg hot rolled | Not economical for one-off prototypes |
| Tooling investment | EUR 3K-20K per new die/roll set | Requires volume commitment to amortize |
| Length constraint | Max 12m hot rolled, 6m cold drawn | Longer continuous pieces require welding |

sections.title: Value Proposition Hierarchy
sections.content:
For capability platforms, the value proposition operates at three levels:

| Level | Statement | Target Audience |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| Strategic | "Replace machined bar stock with near-net-shape profiles to cut material cost 35-60% and improve part performance" | C-level, procurement directors |
| Technical | "Custom cross-section geometry with +/-0.02mm tolerance, controlled hardness to 64 HRC, and grain flow aligned to load path" | Engineering, quality managers |
| Commercial | "Amortize tooling over 12-24 months, then ongoing per-meter pricing with 4-6 week delivery on repeat orders" | Purchasing, supply chain |
